title: DO NOT MERGE: WIP: Revert "vendor containers/image v1.4"
body:
This reverts commit c069d117594d72159157aa48d0693d8571be45c5.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* To fully approve this pull request, please assign additional approvers. We suggest the following additional approver: **mrunalp** If they are not already assigned, you can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @mrunalp` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon @vrothberg I have no idea why, but this was the commit where the +10min slowdown on all OS began.  Testing here to see if reverting gains back that time.  If not, I'll throw the PR away.
Note that reverting this commit will yield broken progress bars. If this update is truly responsible, I suggest bumping the timeouts as a symptomatic patch until we figured out the root cause.

*note:* [Ubuntu failure](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5954628865753088) in [first run is a flake (http timeout)](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5735403366645760)

Baseline timings:
* F29 - 27
* F28 - 26
* Ubu - 38
* RHEL - 36

Timings w/ c069d117594d72159157aa48d0693d8571be45c5. reverted:
* F29 - 30:20
* F28 - 27:21
* RHEL - 33:27
* Ubu - 41:59  (normally +10m slower)

Going through (manually) the history of post-merge test results, this looks to be the history:

1. [Merge of PR #2321 appears to be the last one matching baseline.](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/4867130735460352)
2. [Merge of PR #2336 appears to have made results worse only on Ubuntu](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5658814322835456)
3. [Merge of PR #2332 appears to have made results worse on F29](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6560841089417216)
4. [Merge of PR #2295 appears to have made results worse on F28](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5142407252279296)
5. [Merge of PR 2394 was final nail in coffin#](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5197794982756352)

This is all based on "bisecting" the [chain of master merges reported by cirrus](https://cirrus-ci.com/github/containers/libpod/master).  Clicking on them, and eyeballing the times vs the baseline.  The results may vary +/- a PR depending on which way you tilt your head.

Conclusion: Adding features and fixing bugs appears to grows code, and makes crap run slower :frowning_face:   I'm not sure anyone could look at those PRs and pick with certainty what the "smoldering weapon" was (w/o spending oodles of time).  Consider if time is best spent on that, or if more "explosions for currency-units" would be gained by more controlled performance analysis for each platform.  IMHO, the later probably has a bigger RoI in the long-term.
Thanks for the great analyses, @cevich! I think we could really need some more profiling and dedicate some time to improving the speed of the code.

@vrothberg I only wish I could be more accurate.  We're really starting to hurt in the data gathering / tracking department.  I should probably drop an official TODO item or some thinking on HOWTO for that.
